headline: Arm unveils new AI chip, expects it to add billions in annual revenue
summary: Arm Holdings announced a new artificial intelligence data center chip on Tuesday which it said will add billions of dollars of revenue and ‌represent a significant shift in the company's strategy.  So-called agentic AI has jump-started demand for the central processing units (CPUs) produced by the likes of Intel and Advanced Micro Devices.  After shares jumped following Arm's new chip announcement, the stock reversed course and fell 1.5% in mid-afternoon trading.

headline: Arm releases first in-house chip, with Meta as debut customer
summary: Arm is making its first CPU, pivoting from only licensing its architecture to giants like Apple, Nvidia, Amazon and Google. CNBC got an exclusive first look.

raw.headline: Arm releases first in-house chip, with Meta as debut customer
raw.summary: Arm is making its first CPU, pivoting from only licensing its architecture to giants like Apple, Nvidia, Amazon and Google. CNBC got an exclusive first look.

headline: Inside Arm’s $71 million chip lab where its making its first ever CPU
summary: For more than 35 years, Arm has licensed its chip architecture and collected royalties on every processor made by customers like Apple, Nvidia, Google, Amazon, Intel and Microsoft. Now Arm is becoming their competitor, making physical silicon of its own for the first time, with Meta as the initial customer. CNBC got an exclusive first look at Arm's new AGI CPU that's "ruthlessly optimized" for running AI inference in data centers, and toured the $71 million Austin lab Arm built for its new CPU.

CNBC got an exclusive first look at Arm's new AGI CPU that's "ruthlessly optimized" for running AI inference in data centers, and toured the $71 million Austin lab Arm built for its new CPU.

headline: Musk’s Terafab Fever Dream Exposes Reality of the AI Chip Crunch

headline: AMD AI Deal Talks With Upstage Highlight Valuation Gap And Growth Story
summary: AMD, ticker NasdaqGS:AMD, is in advanced talks to supply around 10,000 MI355 AI accelerators to South Korean AI company Upstage. The potential deal would support South Korea's national AI infrastructure and reduce reliance on a single supplier. This agreement would mark a meaningful AI hardware win for AMD outside the US and expand its international presence in the AI accelerator market. The news follows recent engagement by AMD CEO Lisa Su with Korean tech leaders. For you as an investor...
